Book never seems to make progress

So I have been writing my first book. I've done some planning and got started, set a word goal of 60000 words and writing for about an hour daily. That means I reach around 1000-2000 words daily. I'm done with 26% of the first draft, but I don't feel like I have made any progress at all. I keep writing everyday and although I know I'm making progress (about 2% daily), I know at the current pace I'll be done in 3 weeks, it just doesn't feel like I'm getting closer. I guess maybe this is because there are no built in "checkpoints" or "milestone rewards" unlike other fields and in learning new stuff. For me I think the only time I will feel relief and that satisfaction will be once I finish my first draft. Anyways, I'd love to know what y'all think.

ELI5: Why don't the gas giants just float away into space?

We always hear that planets like Jupiter and Saturn are basically just giant balls of "gas" and clouds with no solid ground to stand on. If they don't have a hard rocky middle like Earth does to hold everything together, why doesn't all that gas just drift off into the vacuum of space?

Is there a point where the gas stops being "air" and starts acting like a solid, or is gravity just that much stronger when you're that big? I'm trying to wrap my head around how something can be both a "cloud" and a heavy planet at the same time.

I’m querying a YA/NA book I’ve been working on for more than a decade and got a rejection email from an agent today that was so unintentionally (I *hope*) mean that it would have made me quit writing forever had I gotten that even a few years ago. These days I collect rejection emails like Pokemon.

A non-publishing person googled me the other day in front of me and said "wow it looks like you're very successful" and a bitter "yeah one would think!!" came out of me unbidden and they looked at me with confusion and alarm and I couldn't figure out how to begin to explain
